mapping.a_element: critical point separating high-temperature normal state and low-temperature state with bimodal density distribution
mapping.b_element: resonance frequencies and altered Rabi frequency separating different regimes of scattered light spectrum

mapping.a_element: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless (BKT) transition in a 2D Bose gas
mapping.b_element: parametric oscillator behavior in a two-level quantum system

mapping.a_element: interaction strength modifying the critical atom number
mapping.b_element: strength of coupling electromagnetic field controlling the Rabi frequency

mapping.a_element: local density approximation combining mean-field theory with BKT transition
mapping.b_element: theoretical demonstration of parametric oscillator behavior under strong electromagnetic fields

brief_justification: While both abstracts describe critical transitions in quantum systems (a phase transition in a Bose gas and resonance behavior in a two-level system), the underlying mechanisms differ fundamentally. Paper A involves a topological phase transition (BKT) driven by vortex-antivortex unbinding in a 2D system, whereas Paper B describes parametric oscillation and Rabi frequency modulation in a driven two-level quantum system. The mathematical structures (e.g., mean-field + BKT vs. driven oscillator equations) are distinct.
